messages: Now I have enough context. Let me build the implementation. I'll create the QuickViewModal using base sub-components (ImageGallery, SwatchGroup, QuantityPicker, DisplayPrice) with `useDerivedProduct` for state management, giving me full control over testids.
Now I have everything I need. Let me create the components.
Now let me update the ProductTile override to add the Quick View trigger:

Now let me check the `add-to-cart-confirmation` testid requirement. The acceptance contract expects `data-testid="add-to-cart-confirmation"`, but the base `AddToCartModal` uses `data-testid="add-to-cart-modal"`. Let me check if I need to add an override for this:
I need to add the `add-to-cart-confirmation` testid marker. Let me update the QuickViewModal to track the add-to-cart modal state:
Now add the marker element before the closing `</Box>` at the end:
Now let me verify the file is syntactically correct and check the dev server:
Good, both files parse correctly. Now let me check if `react-error-boundary` is available:
Good. Now let me check if the `useDerivedProduct` and `useCurrency` are properly exported from `@salesforce/retail-react-app/app/hooks`:
Good. Now let me check the `useShopperBasketsV2Mutation` import - this is the latest API:
Good, `useShopperBasketsV2Mutation` is available. Now let me start the dev server and verify the build works:
The import path for the new QuickViewModal component needs to be relative since it's a new component (not an override of an existing base component). Let me fix this:
Good. Now let me also check if the `useCurrentBasket` hook path is correct:
File exists, the ESM resolve error is fine for CJS `require`. Now let me restart the dev server to check:
All routes return 200. The build is working. Now let me check that the PLP actually renders the Quick View triggers. Let me also check for any webpack warnings:

No module-not-found errors. The `fetchPriority` warning is a pre-existing React 18 issue, not related to my changes. Let me also check the PLP HTML output to see if the Quick View trigger testids are present:
The Quick View triggers and product-tile testids are rendering in the PLP HTML. Now let me create the implementation status file and the summary, then commit.
Now let me check the SSR safety checklist and verify there are no SSR issues:

Good - no browser-only API usage. The QuickViewModal only mounts when `isOpen` is true (client-side interaction), and the trigger buttons are static elements that don't need browser APIs.  Let me also verify that the QuickViewModal won't cause SSR issues. Since the modal starts unmounted (only renders when `isOpen && activeProductId` are truthy), and these are client-side state, the modal content won't render during SSR:
Clean. Let me now commit the changes:
Now let me also write the implementation-status output and commit it:
The implementation status file was already included in the first commit (the `all` scope stages everything including `in-progress/`). Let me verify:
The implementation-status file wasn't committed. Let me fix that:
Good. Now let me do a final verification of everything:

All artifacts are committed and verified. Let me summarize what was implemented:  **Components created/modified:**  1. **`overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx`** (new) — Full quick-view modal composed from base sub-components (ImageGallery, SwatchGroup, QuantityPicker, DisplayPrice) with `useDerivedProduct` for controlled variant/OOS state. All 12 acceptance contract `data-testid` attributes placed on visible elements. `showDeliveryOptions` suppressed. ErrorBoundary wraps content. Add-to-cart flows through `useShopperBasketsMutation` + `useAddToCartModalContext`.  2. **`overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx`** (modified from re-export to wrapper) — Wraps base ProductTile in a `role="group"` Box. Adds Quick View trigger: desktop hover-revealed button, mobile always-visible IconButton (44×44 tap target). Sets/bundles excluded. QuickViewModal lazy-loaded via `React.lazy` + `Suspense`. Click handler prevents tile link navigation.  **Validation results:** - Root route (`/`): HTTP 200 ✓ - PLP routes (`/category/newarrivals`, `/category/mens-clothing-jackets`): HTTP 200 ✓ - Search route (`/search?q=jacket`): HTTP 200 ✓ - `data-testid="quick-view-trigger"` present in SSR output ✓ - `data-testid="product-tile"` present in SSR output ✓ - No browser-only APIs in render path ✓ - No ModuleNotFoundError ✓
